cases.description: Capability is signed correctly, valid at the verification time, and carries a valid delegation link.
Capability signature is valid, but the token is expired at verification time.
Capability signature is valid, but the token is not yet valid at verification time.
Capability payload was modified after signing, so signature verification fails.
Capability was signed after embedding a delegation link whose own signature no longer matches.
Direct issuance capability with no delegation chain. Signature and time bounds both valid.
Capability with three ToolGrants spanning two tool servers and varied constraints.
Capability whose scope carries only ResourceGrants (no tool grants), targeting file and HTTPS URIs.
Capability scoped exclusively to PromptGrants (exact and wildcard prompt names).
Capability whose scope contains all three grant kinds: tool, resource, and prompt.
Capability with an unconstrained tool grant: no constraints, no invocation cap, no cost cap.
Capability with monetary caps (max_cost_per_invocation and max_total_cost) plus DPoP requirement and governed-intent constraint.
Capability whose chain has exactly four links, equal to the verifier's max_delegation_depth.
Capability whose chain has five links, exceeding the verifier's max_delegation_depth of four. Token signature is valid but chain validation fails.
Capability evaluated at exactly issued_at. Lower bound is inclusive, so the token is valid.
Capability evaluated at exactly expires_at. Upper bound is exclusive, so the token has just expired.
Capability whose grant carries four heterogeneous constraints (DomainGlob, MaxLength, MaxArgsSize, Custom).
Capability with a two-link delegation chain where each link's delegatee matches the next link's delegator and timestamps are monotonic.
Capability whose subject public key was swapped after signing. Body hash drifts, so signature verification fails.
Capability evaluated one second before issued_at. The lower bound is inclusive, so one second earlier is not yet valid.
